title: Authentication, Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Privacy · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds tenant-safe identity and privacy boundary from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of tenant-safe identity and privacy boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Verify configured token issuer, audience, algorithm, time, key rotation, and transport before constructing a minimal principal.
Authorize each command from principal, tenant, role, ownership, relationship, and current resource state inside the application boundary.
Require tenant scope in repository APIs, SQL predicates, unique keys, tests, metrics, exports, and administrative paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Authentication Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6826 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove verified identity, resource authorization, tenant query scope, privacy, and redacted audit evidence; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify configured token issuer, audience, algorithm, time, key rotation, and transport before constructing a minimal principal.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Authentication Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify configured token issuer, audience, algorithm, time, key rotation, and transport before constructing a minimal principal. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Authentication Boundary, keep Feed Go Authentication Boundary connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

State the Feed Go Authentication Boundary preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Go Audit Redaction
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-audit-redaction-1083mp" that uses case 4696 to prove verified identity, resource authorization, tenant query scope, privacy, and redacted audit evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Go Audit Redaction.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Feed Go Audit Redaction into observable performance evidence. For Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Go Audit Redaction, keep Feed Go Audit Redaction connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Go Audit Redaction, name the reader, publisher, revision, Go, source, tenant, URL, feed format, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, API, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Go Audit Redaction precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Go Audit Redaction, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop Go team handles case 3397 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove verified identity, resource authorization, tenant query scope, privacy, and redacted audit evidence; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record actor, decision, resource, outcome, and correlation evidence while redacting tokens, feed content, private URLs, SQL, and sensitive queries.

For Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Go Audit Redaction, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: More audit detail always improves security.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Feed Go Audit Redaction
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6014 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove verified identity, resource authorization, tenant query scope, privacy, and redacted audit evidence; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record actor, decision, resource, outcome, and correlation evidence while redacting tokens, feed content, private URLs, SQL, and sensitive queries.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Feed Go Audit Redaction condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Feed Go Audit Redaction

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only.
